summary: Small two-story rectangular house with stone walls, cobblestone foundation border, rows of horizontal windows on each floor, and a dark reddish stepped gable roof (likely nether brick). A narrow raised perimeter walk of cobblestone surrounds the base. The interior is not visible; footprint centered within a larger cleared dirt plot.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with raised perimeter foundation and pitched stepped gable roof centered on long axis
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long central axis (mirrored front/back and left/right along center)

materials.name: glowstone
materials.role: entrance lighting (small glowing block at door area visible)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: possible stone slabs used under windows or roof edging (uncertain)

elements: rows of horizontal windows on both floors (three windows visible per side)
central entrance on one long side with light block above
cobblestone perimeter low wall/ledge around building
stepped gable roof made of dark brick stairs/slabs
stone brick decorative band between floors
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular footprint roughly 9x17 blocks, elevated on a 1-block-high cobblestone border/ledge around the base.
Construct two stacked full-height stories using stonebrick walls; include a horizontal stonebrick band (2 deep) between first and second floor and a cobblestone band at base.
Place three evenly spaced 1x2 (width x height) window openings along each long side per floor, filled with glass panes; align windows between floors.
Create a central entrance on one long side with a short recessed doorway and a glowstone or other light block above the door.
Make a symmetrical stepped gable roof along the long axis using dark reddish stairs (nether_brick stairs) with stone slab or stonebrick corner caps where the steps meet the walls.
uncertainties: Exact interior layout, ceiling heights, and presence of internal floors/stairs are not visible.
Precise block types for some decorative bands and caps (stone slab vs stonebrick slab vs smooth stone) are uncertain.
Width/depth/height are estimated from visible block counts in images; actual footprint may differ by a few blocks.
Material identified as glowstone for entrance lighting is not certain; could be another light-emitting block.
